# agentmem Spec — Drift Anchor
*Generated by PlanForge on 2026-04-06. Not a deliverable — specdrift reference only.*

## Problem
Agent developers reimplement the same memory primitives for every project. paia-memory is the proven solution but is paia-coupled with ad-hoc background jobs. No generic, pluggable, composable version exists.

## Solution
Five-layer Python library (`agentmem`): Core protocols → Adapters (postgres+pgvector, sqlite-vec, ollama, openai, hash, webhook, pg_listen) → Workers (coordinator + embed_reindex, digest, retention, active_context jobs) → Service (FastAPI: Ingest/Retrieval/Admin) → CLI (`am` command). paia-memory becomes a thin adapter on top.

## Out of Scope
- LLM-generated digest summaries (deterministic only in v1)
- Cross-tenant federation at network level
- UI or dashboard, managed cloud, MCP server
- Auth beyond token-based admin guard
- Redis Stream and Cohere adapters in Phase 1

## Success Criteria
- `pip install agentmem[postgres,ollama,cli]` + config produces a running memory service with no other code
- `am ingest evidence` and `am retrieve semantic` work from shell with JSON output
- paia-memory passes its full test suite after refactoring to use agentmem
- Worker coordinator runs all background jobs on schedule without manual cron
- `agentmem[sqlite,hash,cli]` works with zero external services

## Key Constraints
- Python 3.12+, uv, psycopg[binary]+psycopg-pool, pgvector, fastapi, typer, ruff, mypy
- Core layer: zero concrete dependencies (protocols only)
- Domain services accept protocol-typed adapters at construction
- Every API endpoint requires tenant_id; single/multi tenant mode in config
- Embeddings stored ONLY in VectorStore — not as columns on evidence/facet rows
- Worker coordinator persists job state to storage (not in-memory only)

## Dissenting Concerns
- Five layers adds structural complexity — mitigated by clear dependency direction
- PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY adapter ties continuous jobs to Postgres
- In-memory storage adapter needed for unit tests adds maintenance surface
